name: unicode_near_match_distinct_enum
description: Negative near-match (no over-claim): enum ƛ (U+019B) | λ (U+03BB). Input Λ (U+039B) folds to λ (U+03BB) and matches the λ value — it does NOT spuriously fold onto the visually similar ƛ (U+019B). #555 Slice 2 — native now CLAIMS this non-ASCII match_string case (match_string folds through internal/debaml/bamlunicode, byte-for-byte Rust 1.93.0 str::to_lowercase / unicode-normalization 0.1.24, so the verdict is native's to claim, never case-fold-uncertain). want is BAML v0.223's byte-exact final parse, held by this differential; native==BAML is independently proven by the exhaustive bamlunicode Rust reference.
